title: Vaccinated vs. Unvaccinated: Europe’s Covid Culture War
content: In pockets of Europe, vaccine resistance has become the long tail of the populist nationalist movements that shook up European politics for a decade.
en_title: Vaccinated vs. Unvaccinated: Europe’s Covid Culture War
en_content: In pockets of Europe, vaccine resistance has become the long tail of the populist nationalist movements that shook up European politics for a decade.

title: Mass Detentions of Civilians Fan ‘Climate of Fear’ in Ethiopia
content: An ethnically motivated detention campaign largely targeting Ethiopians of Tigrayan descent threatens to further unravel Africa’s second-most populous country a year into a civil war.
en_title: Mass Detentions of Civilians Fan ‘Climate of Fear’ in Ethiopia
en_content: An ethnically motivated detention campaign largely targeting Ethiopians of Tigrayan descent threatens to further unravel Africa’s second-most populous country a year into a civil war.

title: A Green Light at the Window for Migrants
content: An unofficial network of local residents, activists and volunteer medics in Poland has been working to support those who have made it across from Belarus in freezing conditions.

des_facet: Middle East and Africa Migrant Crisis

en_title: A Green Light at the Window for Migrants
en_content: An unofficial network of local residents, activists and volunteer medics in Poland has been working to support those who have made it across from Belarus in freezing conditions.

title: Greece to Put Aid Workers Who Helped Migrants on Trial on Espionage Charges
content: The country’s conservative government is toughening its stance on migration and on groups working with migrants, aligning itself with a hardening climate across Europe.

des_facet: Middle East and Africa Migrant Crisis

en_title: Greece to Put Aid Workers Who Helped Migrants on Trial on Espionage Charges
en_content: The country’s conservative government is toughening its stance on migration and on groups working with migrants, aligning itself with a hardening climate across Europe.

title: An Australian’s Search for Belonging Led to the Silk Road and a Famed Hat
content: For an Australian woman with roots in Central Asia, a growing awareness of her heritage and the peril her people face all began with an embroidered skullcap.
en_title: An Australian’s Search for Belonging Led to the Silk Road and a Famed Hat
en_content: For an Australian woman with roots in Central Asia, a growing awareness of her heritage and the peril her people face all began with an embroidered skullcap.

title: U.S.-China Summit Produces Little More Than Polite Words, but They Help
content: In a chilly relationship of mutual mistrust, staving off the prospect of a broader conflict between two superpowers counts as progress.

more_info.byline: By Steven Lee Myers and David E. Sanger

en_title: U.S.-China Summit Produces Little More Than Polite Words, but They Help
en_content: In a chilly relationship of mutual mistrust, staving off the prospect of a broader conflict between two superpowers counts as progress.

title: China’s History Is Revised, to the Glory of Xi Jinping
content: The Communist Party has anointed him one of its revered leaders, paving the way for an already powerful leader to wield even greater influence.
en_title: China’s History Is Revised, to the Glory of Xi Jinping
en_content: The Communist Party has anointed him one of its revered leaders, paving the way for an already powerful leader to wield even greater influence.

title: Cold and Marooned in a Police State as Desperation Takes Hold
content: Almost a dozen migrants have died in recent weeks in the standoff between Belarus, a close ally of Russia, and Poland, a member of NATO and the European Union, each determined not to bend.

more_info.byline: By Andrew Higgins and Marc Santora
des_facet: Middle East and Africa Migrant Crisis

en_content: Almost a dozen migrants have died in recent weeks in the standoff between Belarus, a close ally of Russia, and Poland, a member of NATO and the European Union, each determined not to bend.

title: Liverpool Bomber Had Planned for Months, U.K. Police Say
content: The authorities named the man they believe to be behind an explosion in a taxi outside a hospital, who was also the only person killed, as Emad Al Swealmeen, 32.
en_title: Liverpool Bomber Had Planned for Months, U.K. Police Say
en_content: The authorities named the man they believe to be behind an explosion in a taxi outside a hospital, who was also the only person killed, as Emad Al Swealmeen, 32.

title: In Gaza, a Contentious Palestinian Professor Calmly Teaches Israeli Poetry
content: On social media, Refaat Alareer rages against Israel. In the lecture hall, he studiously analyzes the work of some of its leading poets — and surprises some of his students.
en_title: In Gaza, a Contentious Palestinian Professor Calmly Teaches Israeli Poetry
en_content: On social media, Refaat Alareer rages against Israel. In the lecture hall, he studiously analyzes the work of some of its leading poets — and surprises some of his students.

title: Nigerian Army Killed Unarmed Protesters, Report Finds
content: The incident in October 2020 could be “equated with a massacre,” a government panel said, refuting the army’s claim that soldiers only fired blanks.
en_title: Nigerian Army Killed Unarmed Protesters, Report Finds
en_content: The incident in October 2020 could be “equated with a massacre,” a government panel said, refuting the army’s claim that soldiers only fired blanks.

title: Woman’s Body Recovered From Mudslide in British Columbia as Evacuations Continue
content: “It came down faster than the speed of sound,” said a woman who survived the mudslide. Heavy rainfall and overflowing rivers have inundated cities in the Canadian province.

more_info.byline: By Christine Hauser, Christine Chung and Alyssa Lukpat

en_title: Woman’s Body Recovered From Mudslide in British Columbia as Evacuations Continue
en_content: “It came down faster than the speed of sound,” said a woman who survived the mudslide. Heavy rainfall and overflowing rivers have inundated cities in the Canadian province.

title: Boris Johnson Battered in Parliament as Scandal Puts Him on Defense
content: Reversals and broken promises have eroded his party’s lead in the polls, and the prime minister faces a low-grade mutiny from Conservatives angry over a proposal to limit their options for extra income.

more_info.byline: By Mark Landler and Stephen Castle

en_title: Boris Johnson Battered in Parliament as Scandal Puts Him on Defense
en_content: Reversals and broken promises have eroded his party’s lead in the polls, and the prime minister faces a low-grade mutiny from Conservatives angry over a proposal to limit their options for extra income.

title: How the Belarus Standoff Is Unlike Recent Migrant Crises
content: The images at the Belarus-Poland border look familiar, but this emergency has little in common with previous waves of people from Syria, Myanmar and Afghanistan.

title: Raúl Rivero, Disenchanted Poet of the Cuban Revolution, Dies at 75
content: A leading journalistic voice who broke with the Castro regime, he gained wide recognition for his protests, was jailed as a dissident and went into exile.
en_title: Raúl Rivero, Disenchanted Poet of the Cuban Revolution, Dies at 75
en_content: A leading journalistic voice who broke with the Castro regime, he gained wide recognition for his protests, was jailed as a dissident and went into exile.

title: The Netherlands is maxing out its coronavirus testing capacity.
content: Soaring demand and a shortage of workers have made appointments almost impossible to find.

title: A Syrian teenager is buried in Poland, and a pair of brothers are rescued from a forest.

title: An injured Olympic athlete was flown home on a cargo plane because of Covid rules in Beijing.
content: A Polish luger, Mateusz Sochowicz, was told that coronavirus restrictions prevented him from boarding a commercial flight for another two weeks.

title: Italian Police Break Up Drug Smuggling Ring Tied to Calabria Mob

title: Will an Island in Indonesia Become a New Frontier in the Space Race?
